stories.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
stories.summary: A ceasefire between the US and Iran has been in place since April 8. Both sides are yet to agree on a peace deal.

stories.headline: Iran war day 70: US, Iran trade fire in Hormuz amid ceasefire tensions
stories.summary: Iran accuses US of breaching ceasefire near Hormuz as CENTCOM says forces responded to Iranian attacks.

stories.headline: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iranian Targets; Iran Says It Returned Fire
stories.summary: The exchange was the latest twist in a week of mixed signals in the region and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump searches for an off-ramp in the war that he started.

stories.headline: Russia, Ukraine trade fire, blame despite Victory Day ceasefire

stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating the truce, alleging it targeted an oil tanker and carried out attacks on coastal areas.

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaching Victory Day ceasefire
stories.summary: The two sides report responding to hundreds of drone attacks, just hours into a truce to cover celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

stories.headline: Aramco, Adnoc Sneak Oil Through Hormuz as Iran Menaces Strait
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s Aramco Trading Co. and the UAE’s state oil company Adnoc are among firms that have moved crude cargoes through the Strait of Hormuz since Iran effectively closed the waterway, according to people familiar with the situation.

stories.headline: US Says Iran Should Give Answer Friday as Clashes Raise Tensions
stories.summary: The US said it expects Iran to respond to President Donald Trump’s latest proposal to end their war imminently, as overnight clashes threatened to further fracture a monthlong ceasefire.

stories.summary: Bloc is expected to call for deeper energy cooperation and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling

stories.summary: A Malta-flagged tanker, carrying one million barrels of crude oil, has successfully reached the western coast of South Korea after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.headline: Gundlach Takes Longshot Bet in Case of US Debt Restructuring
stories.summary: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach is repositioning some of his funds for the extreme scenario that the US government could choose to restructure its debt in response to a potential future recession.

stories.headline: UK elections – early results and takeaways; will Starmer have to resign?

Leo XIV, Pope's anniversary in Pompeii and Naples: 'We cannot resign ourselves to death'
stories.summary: The Pontiff is on a pastoral visit to Campania, where he relaunched the call for peace that had marked the first words of his papacy. "The disarming and disarming peace" of a year ago in St Peter's Square became a prayer: "May God enlighten those who have responsibility for government".

stories.headline: Third British national has suspected hantavirus infection, UK government says

stories.headline: Treasuries Gain After Mixed Jobs Data Cements Bets on Steady Fed
stories.summary: Treasuries gained after a mixed batch of US employment data cemented the market’s view that the Federal Reserve will be cautious to change monetary policy.

stories.headline: Enhanced Games Falls 14% After SPAC Merger at $1.2 Billion Value
stories.summary: Enhanced Group Inc. shares sank 14% after going public via a merger with blank-check company A Paradise Acquisition Corp. that gave it an enterprise value of $1.2 billion.

stories.headline: Online Mob Fuels 6,000% Stock Rally in Obscure SpaceX Rival
stories.summary: Championed by a guru known simply as the Kook, the satellite company AST has become one of the world's most expensive stocks.

stories.headline: BlackRock's Rieder Says Equities Aren't in a Bubble, Fed on Hold
stories.summary: Rick Rieder, BlackRock's CIO of global fixed income, says US equities are not in a bubble because of capital expenditures and earnings growth on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Jobs Data ‘Steady as It Goes,’ Says BlackRock’s Rosenberg
stories.summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, portfolio manager of the Systemic Multi-Strategy Fund at BlackRock, reacts to the April US jobs report as he sees a trade-off in the data between a strong headline number and weakness in income. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fed Data Suggest Japan Sold US Debt Amid Intervention
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve’s custody holdings of Treasuries fell for the first time in a month at a time Japan was likely intervening to support its currency, with market participants debating whether the nation offloaded US securities to fund its yen purchases.

stories.headline: Turkey Wheat Output Seen Rebounding After Drought-Hit 2025
stories.summary: Turkey’s wheat harvest is poised for a sharp recovery from last year’s crop-ravaging droughts, potentially curbing the outlook for imports from one of the world’s largest wheat buyers.

stories.headline: UK Bonds Rally as Starmer Says He’ll Stay as Prime Minister
stories.summary: UK government bonds rallied as Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to remain in his job despite his party’s election losses.

stories.headline: Pentagon Releases Files on U.F.O.s
stories.summary: The initial files are murky images that show what could be anything. The government said more would be released on a rolling basis.

stories.headline: Rubio Meets Meloni as U.S.-Italy Relations Strained and Trump’s Attacks on Pope

stories.headline: In L.A. Mayor’s Race, Karen Bass Faces Vocal Rivals and a ‘Disgruntled Electorate’
stories.summary: The mayor of Los Angeles is running for a second term, as a former ally and a reality TV star tap into widespread voter angst.

stories.headline: Commerzbank axes 3,000 jobs in an attempt to fight off UniCredit takeover bid
stories.summary: Commerzbank wants to cut a further 3,000 jobs and at the same time significantly increase its profit targets. The reorganisation is the bank's response to the takeover ambitions of the major Italian bank UniCredit.

stories.headline: U.S. intelligence says Iran can outlast Trump’s Hormuz blockade for months

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.headline: Stocks Rise as Jobs Remain Strong in Wake of War: Markets Wrap

stories.headline: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
